claims.claim: Heebo, Assistant, Rubik, Frank Ruhl Libre, and Secular One are Hebrew fonts on Google Fonts; their Latin pairings (Inter, Source Sans 3, Roboto, Merriweather, Montserrat) are also on Google Fonts. Source Sans Pro was renamed Source Sans 3 (Adobe, 2021) and Google Fonts dropped the old name.
claims.raw_snippet: Hebrew fonts on Google Fonts include Heebo, Assistant, Rubik, Frank Ruhl Libre, and Secular One. Source Sans 3 (formerly Source Sans Pro) is available on Google Fonts.

claims.claim: The Google Fonts css2 endpoint loads Heebo and Inter with font-display: swap via an @import or link.
claims.raw_snippet: The css2 endpoint returns @font-face rules for the requested families (Heebo, Inter) with the requested weights and display=swap.

claims.claim: CSS logical properties (padding-inline, margin-block, inset-inline-start) provide direction-aware layout and are documented by MDN.
claims.raw_snippet: CSS logical properties and values provide the ability to control layout through logical, rather than physical, direction and dimension mappings.

claims.claim: Tailwind provides rtl: and ltr: variants for direction-specific component styling.
claims.raw_snippet: Use the rtl and ltr variants to conditionally apply styles in right-to-left and left-to-right modes.

claims.claim: shadcn/ui provides RTL-friendly component recipes and primitives.
claims.raw_snippet: shadcn/ui is a set of accessible, composable React component recipes built on Radix and Tailwind.

claims.claim: WCAG AA requires at least 4.5:1 contrast for normal text and 3:1 for large text and UI components; encode these into design tokens.
claims.raw_snippet: WCAG success criterion 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum) requires a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 for normal text, and 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast requires 3:1 for UI components and graphical objects.

claims.claim: The Israeli Government Design System (IGDS) is published as a Figma Community file ('IGDS Design System File 2.0') used to unify gov.il user experience.
claims.raw_snippet: IGDS Design System File 2.0 on Figma Community: the Israeli Government Design System with tokens, components, and RTL Hebrew illustrations.

claims.claim: Intl.NumberFormat('he-IL', { style: 'currency', currency: 'ILS' }) formats shekel amounts (e.g. 1,234.50 with the U+20AA sign), and the shekel sign is not a directional character so inline amounts need bidi isolation.
claims.raw_snippet: new Intl.NumberFormat('he-IL', { style: 'currency', currency: 'ILS' }).format(1234.5) returns a shekel-formatted string such as 1,234.50 with the new shekel sign; both '1,234.50 ₪' (finance) and '₪ 1,234.50' (retail) orderings occur.

claims.claim: The CSS oklch() color function is Baseline (widely available) since 2023, enabling perceptually-uniform color ramps.
claims.raw_snippet: The oklch() functional notation expresses a color in the Oklch color space; it is Baseline widely available across browsers.
